rationale: M1-M6 already produced (smoke + prior 2 normal runs). This run resumes from M7 per prior summary's next_run_target. Target: complete M7-M12 — Integration (Unit 6 split a/b), DiffEq (Unit 7), Applications of Integration (Unit 8 split a/b), and the AP exam synthesis module. CB topic mappings come from references/ap-calc-ab-ced.md 'Suggested GIIS Module Mapping' table.

modules_planned_this_run.title: Riemann Sums and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules_planned_this_run.weighting: part of 17-20% MC (Unit 6 is the heaviest)
common_traps: Sign errors between ∫ from a to b vs ∫ from b to a
Confusing the accumulation function F(x) = ∫ from a to x f(t)dt's derivative with f(x) (mis-stating FTC Part 1)
Forgetting that the definite integral is a NUMBER (signed area), not a function

modules_planned_this_run.title: Antiderivatives and Integration by Substitution
modules_planned_this_run.weighting: part of 17-20% MC (Unit 6 is the heaviest)
common_traps: Substitution: forgetting to change dx to du AND update the bounds on definite integrals
Mixing up which factor is u vs du (the chain-rule pattern)

modules_planned_this_run.title: Differential Equations: Slope Fields and Separation of Variables
common_traps: Forgetting +C BEFORE applying the initial condition (you can't fold C into y once the IC is plugged in)
Algebra errors when separating variables — dividing by an expression that could be zero, losing solutions
Misinterpreting slope-field arrows (they show dy/dx at each point, not y itself)

modules_planned_this_run.title: Average Value and Area Between Curves
common_traps: Average value formula: dividing by (b−a), NOT multiplying
Area between curves: forgetting 'top minus bottom' orientation (or 'right minus left' when integrating in y)
Curves that intersect more than twice — splitting the integral at each crossing and absolute-value-ing each piece

modules_planned_this_run.title: Volumes: Cross-Sections, Discs, and Washers
common_traps: Disc vs washer: forgetting the inner-radius (the hole) for the washer method
Revolving around y = k (k ≠ 0): forgetting to shift the radii by k
Mixing up axis of revolution and bounds of integration (integrate in dx if revolving around a horizontal axis using vertical slices)

modules_planned_this_run.title: AP Exam Synthesis and Free-Response Strategy
cb_topics: FRQ patterns (rate-in/rate-out, particle motion, area-volume, table-based, differential equation, function-defined-by-integral)
common_traps: Forgetting units on contextual answers (FRQ graders deduct for missing/incorrect units)
Failing to label 'so f has a local max at x = c BECAUSE f' changes from + to −' — justification points require the BECAUSE
Calculator section: not storing intermediate values and re-typing decimals, accumulating rounding error

throughput_strategy: Spawn 6 end-to-end module producer sub-agents — each runs its own internal generator + 3-reviewer cascade + lesson-writer pipeline for ONE module. Batched 4 + 2 to respect AUTO_PIPELINE's 4-concurrent limit.
